/changelog/lending-borrowing-dashboard.title: Lending & Borrowing Dashboard - SquareDocs
p: Lending & Borrowing Page - Dedicated dashboard for all lending positions
Health Factor Monitoring - Real-time liquidation risk assessment with visual indicators
LTV Speedometer - Intuitive gauge showing your loan-to-value ratio
Multi-Protocol Support - Aave V2 and V3 tracking across all chains
Risk Metrics - Max borrow capacity, safe withdrawal amounts, and liquidation prices
Collateral Breakdown - See exactly what assets are securing your loans
Position grouping by protocol for better organization
Automatic health factor alerts when approaching liquidation thresholds
Historical APY tracking for supplied and borrowed assets
Mobile-optimized lending dashboard with touch-friendly controls
Fixed health factor calculations showing incorrect values for multi-collateral positions
Resolved issue where borrowed stablecoins weren't aggregating correctly
Corrected supply APY display for rebasing tokens like stETH

/static/privacy-policy.title: Privacy Policy - SquareDocs
/static/privacy-policy.description: We respect your privacy and are committed to protecting your personal data. This Privacy Policy explains how we collect, use, and safeguard your information when you use our website. By accessing our site, you agree to this policy.
p: Welcome to [Your Website Name]. We respect your privacy and are committed to protecting your personal data. This Privacy Policy explains how we collect, use, and safeguard your information when you use our website. By accessing our site, you agree to this policy.
Personal Information: Name, email address, phone number, payment details (if applicable), and other identifiers you voluntarily provide.
Non-Personal Information: Browser type, IP address, device information, cookies, and usage data (e.g., pages visited).
Analyze website traffic and user behavior.
We do not sell your personal data. We may share it with:
Service Providers: Third parties (e.g., payment processors, hosting providers) who assist in operating our website.
Legal Requirements: If required by law or to protect our rights.
Depending on your location, you may have the right to:
Access, correct, or delete your data.
We use cookies to enhance user experience. You can disable cookies via your browser settings, but this may affect site functionality.
We implement industry-standard measures to protect your data. However, no online transmission is 100% secure.
Data may be transferred to and processed in countries outside your residence. We ensure adequate safeguards are in place.
Our website is not intended for users under [13/16] years old. We do not knowingly collect data from minors.
We may update this policy periodically. The latest version will be posted here

/static/terms-of-service.title: Terms of Service - SquareDocs
/static/terms-of-service.description: By accessing or using SquareDocs, you agree to comply with these Terms of Service. If you disagree, do not use the site.
p: By accessing or using SquareDocs, you agree to comply with these Terms of Service. If you disagree, do not use the site.
Provide accurate information when creating an account.
Do not misuse the website (e.g., hacking, spamming).
All content (text, images, logos) on this site is owned by SquareDocs or licensors. Unauthorized use is prohibited.
The website is provided “as is” without warranties of any kind.
We do not guarantee uninterrupted or error-free service.
You agree to indemnify us against claims arising from your misuse of the site.
We reserve the right to suspend or terminate access for violations of these terms.
These terms are governed by the laws of USA
We may update these terms at any time. Continued use constitutes acceptance.

/founding-charter-members.title: Founding & Charter Members - DeFi Orbit Docs
/founding-charter-members.description: DeFi Orbit recognizes and honors our earliest believers—the users who had faith in our vision before the masses arrived. These special membership tiers celebrate the people who supported us from the beginning.
p: DeFi Orbit recognizes and honors our earliest believers—the users who had faith in our vision before the masses arrived. These special membership tiers celebrate the people who supported us from the beginning.
We've created two exclusive membership tiers for our first 100 paid customers:
The ultimate early believers who pre-paid annually
Early supporters who joined on monthly subscriptions
Both tiers come with exclusive benefits, special recognition, and privileges that honor your early commitment to DeFi Orbit.
You're a Founding Member if you meet ALL of these requirements:
One of the first 100 annually-paid customers
Founding Members showed the ultimate commitment by investing in DeFi Orbit's annual plan before we were fully proven in the market.
Once you're a Founding Member, these benefits are yours for life:

✅ Golden checkmark badge visible throughout the platform✅ Subtle gold accent glow behind your avatar✅ Unique profile badge distinguishing you from other users✅ "Founding Member" status displayed in Account Settings✅ Recognition in future community interactions

✅ Your plan pricing is locked at the rate you paid—forever✅ Lock transfers automatically to each new annual period✅ Never pay more than your original Founding Member rate✅ Keep your price lock until you voluntarily cancel
✅ Private Founding Members Discord channel✅ Feature voting with 2x weight on our roadmap✅ Up to two weeks early access to all new features✅ First in line for new swag and merchandise when available
✅ Listed on our public "Founding Members" page✅ Annual anniversary email acknowledging your support✅ Recognized as a core part of DeFi Orbit's origin story
You're a Charter Member if you meet ALL of these requirements:
One of the first 100 paid customers
Maintain an active subscription to keep benefits
Charter Members joined early and believed in us, choosing to support us monthly as we grew.
These benefits remain active as long as you maintain your subscription:
✅ Silver checkmark badge throughout the platform✅ Special visual treatment (one tier below Founding Members)✅ "Charter Member" status displayed in Account Settings✅  Recognition as an early believer

✅ Your pricing is locked at your original signup rate✅ Lock remains active as long as your subscription continues✅ Important: If you cancel and return later, you'll pay current market rates✅ Incentive to maintain your subscription and keep your locked pricing
✅ 1-week early access to new features (versus 2 weeks for Founding Members)✅ Feature voting with 1.5x weight on our roadmap✅ Priority consideration for beta testing opportunities

Honoring Early Believers The first 100 customers took a chance on DeFi Orbit before we were proven. You believed in our vision, gave us feedback that shaped the product, and supported us financially when it mattered most.
Building Community Founding and Charter Members aren't just customers—you're part of DeFi Orbit's foundation. These tiers recognize that contribution and give you a voice in how we evolve.

Price Protection We want to reward your early commitment. As DeFi Orbit grows and adds features, prices may increase for new users. Your locked pricing ensures you'll never pay more than your original rate.
What happens if I cancel my Charter Membership? You'll lose your Charter Member status and price lock. If you return later, you'll pay current market rates and won't regain Charter Member benefits.

Do Founding Members keep their status if they cancel? Your Founding Member status and benefits are tied to maintaining an active subscription. If you cancel and return, you may lose certain benefits depending on how long you were away. We evaluate these on a case-by-case basis.

Can I opt out of being listed on the Founding Members page? Yes, absolutely. Being listed is opt-in only. You can choose not to be publicly recognized and still keep all other Founding Member benefits.
How do I know if I'm a Founding or Charter Member? Check above your username. You can also check settings, membership tier will be displayed in the "Status" section if you qualify.

Will there be future opportunities to become a Founding or Charter Member? No. These tiers are exclusively for the first 100 paid customers who joined before general availability. This exclusivity is what makes these memberships special.
Any other questions? Get in touch

/updating-your-yield-income-target.title: Updating your Yield Income Target - DeFi Orbit Docs
/updating-your-yield-income-target.description: Change your annual yield income goal anytime to reflect your evolving investment strategy.
h2: How the Yield Income Tracker Works
p: Change your annual yield income goal anytime to reflect your evolving investment strategy.
Navigate to Investor Profile in the main menu
Enter your new annual income target (in USD)
Your dashboard will immediately update to show your progress toward the new goal.
Current Annual Run-Rate Your projected annual income based on current daily yield from in-range positions.
Your Target The annual income goal you've set (e.g., "$10,000 /year")
Progress Indicator Visual bar showing how close you are to reaching your goal, with the exact amount remaining displayed.
Important: Only in-range positions that are actively earning fees count toward your annual run-rate. Out-of-range positions are excluded since they're not generating yield.
Consider your capital If you have $100,000 deployed and target $50,000/year, you'd need to maintain a 50% APR across all positions—which is ambitious.
Account for market conditions Bear markets typically offer lower yields than bull markets. Your target might need adjustment based on overall DeFi market conditions.
Factor in active management Higher targets often require more frequent rebalancing to keep positions in range and maximizing fees.
Start conservative You can always increase your target as your portfolio grows or market opportunities improve.
Focus your strategy Having a clear target helps you make better decisions about position sizing, protocol selection, and risk management.
Measure progress See at a glance whether your current positions are on track to meet your annual income goals.
Adjust as needed Your financial goals change over time. Update your target as your portfolio grows or your income needs shift.
For detailed information about how the Yield Income Tracker appears on your dashboard, see Portfolio Dashboard - Yield Income Tracker.
Any other questions? Get in touch

/risk-profile-assessment.title: Risk Profile Assessment - DeFi Orbit Docs
/risk-profile-assessment.description: Understand your DeFi investing style and get personalized portfolio guidance based on your risk profile.
h2: What You'll See After the Assessment
p: Understand your DeFi investing style and get personalized portfolio guidance based on your risk profile.
The Risk Assessment is a short questionnaire that helps you understand whether your current portfolio aligns with your risk tolerance, investment goals, and management style.
Based on your answers, you'll receive one of three risk profiles:
High-risk, high-return strategies with active management
Balanced approach mixing growth and stability
Capital preservation with steady income focus
Navigate to Investor Profile in the main menu
Click Take Assessment (first time) or Retake Assessment (if updating)
Answer the questionnaire honestly based on your actual investing behavior
You can retake the assessment anytime as your strategy or comfort level evolves.
Once you complete the questionnaire, your results page displays:
Your Risk Profile Type Which investor category you fall into (Aggressive, Moderate, or Conservative) with your total score.
Profile Description A summary of what defines your investing style and primary objectives.
Recommended vs. Current Allocation Side-by-side comparison showing:
Recommended allocation for your risk profile
Percentage gaps highlighting where adjustments may be needed
Strategy Approach Specific recommendations aligned with your risk tolerance:
Key Metrics to Track Performance indicators that matter most for your investing style.
Risk Management Guidelines Specific practices to help you manage risk effectively based on your profile.
Action Items Personalized next steps based on gaps between your current portfolio and recommended allocation. For example:
"Consider moving 15% from growth positions into core holdings"
"Your stablecoin reserves are below recommended levels"
"Your rebalancing frequency is higher than typical for your risk profile"
Copy Results to Clipboard Save your complete risk profile for reference or sharing.
Willing to explore newer protocols and innovations
Frequent portfolio rebalancing (daily or multiple times weekly)
25-30% Core holdings (blue-chip assets in active LP positions)
Key Focus: Daily yield generation, position range status, fee accumulation rate, impermanent loss vs. fees.
Mix of concentrated and wider-range positions
Regular monitoring with less frequent adjustments
Combines established protocols with select new opportunities
Weekly position reviews with occasional adjustments
Key Focus: Weekly yield trends, position health, protocol TVL stability, portfolio weighted APR.
Prioritizes capital preservation and steady income
Focus on lending and wider-range liquidity positions
Less frequent position adjustments (bi-weekly or monthly)
Primary goal: income generation and stability
50-60% Core holdings (blue-chip assets, wide-range LP positions)
Key Focus: Monthly income generated, portfolio stability, protocol security ratings, long-term performance.
Portfolio Alignment Check Compare your actual portfolio allocation to what's recommended for your risk profile. See exactly where you're over or under-allocated.
Actionable Next Steps Get specific, personalized recommendations based on the gaps between your current portfolio and ideal allocation. No guesswork—just clear action items.
Strategy Validation Confirm whether your current approach (rebalancing frequency, protocol selection, position types) matches your stated risk tolerance and goals.

Identify Misalignments Spot potential issues early. For example, if you're classified as Conservative but hold 60% in narrow-range positions, you'll see a clear warning that your portfolio is riskier than you may be comfortable with.
Informed Adjustments Use the side-by-side comparison to make data-driven decisions about rebalancing toward your ideal allocation.
Any other questions? Get in touch

/updating-your-username.title: Updating Your Username - DeFi Orbit Docs
/updating-your-username.description: Change your DeFi Orbit username anytime from your account settings.
p: Change your DeFi Orbit username anytime from your account settings.
Click on your current username in the top navigation
Your new username will be updated immediately across the platform.
Your username must follow these rules:
Must start with a letter or number
Only one user can have each username
If taken, you'll need to choose another
Keep it simple - Shorter usernames are easier to remember and share
Make it unique - Add numbers or underscores if your preferred name is taken
Avoid special characters - Stick to letters, numbers, underscores, and hyphens
Choose wisely - While you can change your username anytime, consistency helps with community recognition
Someone else is using that username. Try adding numbers, underscores, or slight variations.
Remove any spaces, special characters, or symbols. Only use letters, numbers, underscores (_), and hyphens (-).
Reduce your username to 20 characters or fewer.
Must start with letter or number
Usernames cannot start with underscores or hyphens. Start with a letter (a-z, A-Z) or number (0-9) instead.
Any other questions? Get in touch

/lending-borrowing-cards-dashboard.title: Lending & Borrowing Cards (Dashboard) - DeFi Orbit Docs
/lending-borrowing-cards-dashboard.description: Monitor your lending and borrowing positions across protocols with real-time health factor tracking and risk assessment.
p: Monitor your lending and borrowing positions across protocols with real-time health factor tracking and risk assessment.
Click the Lending & Borrowing tab in the position filters
Your view switches from liquidity pools to lending positions
All dashboard metrics update to reflect only lending/borrowing data
Each lending/borrowing position card displays key information about your position on a specific protocol and blockchain.
Protocol name with external link icon
Expand Details - Opens detailed position modal
Open in [Protocol] - Direct link to manage position on the protocol
Protocol where your collateral is supplied.
Total value of supplied assets minus debt
Which connected wallet holds this position
Total USD value of all assets you've supplied
Total USD value of all assets you've borrowed
Your current loan-to-value ratio with visual indicator
How much more you can safely borrow
Your net interest paid or earned per day
The LTV Indicator Visual bar showing your current loan-to-value ratio with three zones:
Your debt is well below maximum limits
Room to borrow more if needed
The closer to red, the riskier your position
High risk of liquidation if prices move against you
The highest LTV ratio your debt can reach as a percentage of your total collateral
This is NOT your liquidation point
The debt-to-collateral ratio at which your position CAN be liquidated
If your LTV reaches this level, liquidators can close your position
Always maintain LTV well below this threshold
View all assets you've supplied to the lending protocol:
Which token you've deposited as collateral
Token amount + USD value you've supplied
Annual percentage yield you're earning on this asset
Loan-to-value ratio for this specific asset (how much you can borrow against it)
Why Supply APY Varies: Different assets have different supply rates based on:
Market demand for borrowing that asset
Higher LTV (e.g., 80%) = Can borrow more against this asset (usually stablecoins or blue chips)
Lower LTV (e.g., 65%) = Can borrow less (riskier or more volatile assets)
View all assets you've borrowed from the protocol:
Token amount + USD value of your debt
Annual interest rate you're paying on this loan
Price at which this specific asset would trigger liquidation
Why Borrow APY Varies: Borrow rates change based on:
If the borrowed asset's price reaches this level, you're at risk of liquidation
Monitor this closely for volatile assets
Multiple borrowed assets = multiple liquidation prices to track
Any other questions? Get in touch

/position-cards-dashboard.title: Position Cards (Dashboard) - DeFi Orbit Docs
/position-cards-dashboard.description: View comprehensive information about individual liquidity positions by clicking on any position card in your dashboard.
p: View comprehensive information about individual liquidity positions by clicking on any position card in your dashboard.
Click on any position card in your "My Positions" section
A detailed modal opens showing complete position information
Click "Close" or outside the modal to return to your dashboard
The protocol where your position is.
Red dot = Out of range (not earning fees) Green dot = In range (actively earning fees)
The two tokens in your liquidity position (e.g., USDC/WETH) with token icons.
The blockchain where this position exists (e.g., Ethereum, Optimism, Solana).
The fee tier for this pool (e.g., 0.30%)
How long you've held this position
Which connected wallet holds this position
Visual representation of your price range with current price indicator
Projected earnings for the week. Toggle between Daily (D), Weekly (W), or Monthly (M) views.
Uncollected fees available to claim right now.
Current annualized return rate for this position.
Your initial capital deployed into this position.
Current value of your position including tokens and uncollected fees.
Your profit or loss on this position.
Which token the yield is in (USDC, WETH, etc.)
All fees earned since position opened (token amount + USD value)
Fees you've already collected (token amount + USD value)
Fees available to claim now (token amount + USD value)
Average daily earnings for this token (token amount + USD value)
For positions that are no longer active, you'll see:
Status Banner "THIS POSITION IS NO LONGER ACTIVE" displayed prominently at the top.
Final profit/loss when position was closed
Amount you withdrew when closing the position
View position on the native protocol interface
Note: This opens a new tab and takes you directly to your position on the protocol's website.
Any other questions? Get in touch

/portfolio-dashboard-pools.title: Portfolio Dashboard (Pools)  - DeFi Orbit Docs
/portfolio-dashboard-pools.description: Your Portfolio Dashboard is the command center for monitoring all your DeFi positions. It provides a real-time overview of your liquidity pools, lending positions, and overall portfolio performance across all supported chains.
h3: CMC Crypto Fear and Greed Index
p: Your Portfolio Dashboard is the command center for monitoring all your DeFi positions. It provides a real-time overview of your liquidity pools, lending positions, and overall portfolio performance across all supported chains.
Note: This page covers the Pools view. For lending and borrowing positions, see Lending & Borrowing Dashboard.

The dashboard automatically updates based on your activity, showing live data for critical metrics like prices, range status, and yields. Everything you need to make informed decisions is visible at a glance.
Total cryptocurrency market capitalization with 24-hour percentage change
Total Value Locked across all DeFi protocols with 24-hour percentage change
These metrics help you understand the broader market context for your investments.
The main dashboard displays your most important portfolio metrics in easy-to-read cards:
Your projected earnings for the week based on current performance. Toggle between Daily (D), Weekly (W), or Monthly (M) views to see different timeframes.
Projected yield earnings based on approximate APR from your active positions.
Total uncollected fees available to claim across all your positions.
Accumulated fees sitting in your positions that you haven't claimed yet. These fees are already yours—you just need to execute a transaction to collect them.
Your current weighted average annual percentage rate across all active positions.
Your current annualized return rate based on fees generated. This only includes positions that are actively earning (in-range liquidity positions).
The total amount of capital you've deployed into DeFi positions.
Your initial deposits across all liquidity pools, measured in USD.
The total amount of principal you've withdrawn from positions, not counting fees or yields collected.
Tracks your capital flow and shows how much of your original investment is still actively deployed.
The current USD value of all your liquidity positions combined.
Current token values + uncollected fees. This is the total value you could withdraw if you closed all positions right now.
Shows how many of your concentrated liquidity positions are currently in-range versus total positions.
Number of in-range positions / Total positions (e.g., "3/4" means 3 out of 4 positions are in-range and actively earning fees).
Your total realized and unrealized gains/losses across all positions.
Position value changes + fees earned - impermanent loss - gas costs. Green indicates profit, red indicates loss.
The total amount of fees you've collected from all positions over time.
Sum of all fee collection transactions you've executed. This is your actual realized income from providing liquidity and does not include uncollected pending yield.
If you set an annual yield income target during signup, you'll see a progress tracker showing:
Your projected annual income based on current daily yield: Daily Yield × 365 days
The annual income goal you set (e.g., "$10,000 /year")
Visual indicator showing how close you are to reaching your goal.
Important: Only in-range positions that are actively earning fees count toward your annual run-rate. Out-of-range positions are excluded from this calculation since they're not generating yield.
You can update your yield income target anytime from your account settings.
On the right side of your dashboard, you'll find market sentiment tools:
A visual gauge showing current market sentiment from "Extreme Fear" to "Extreme Greed."
Red zone (0-25): Extreme Fear - Market may be oversold
Orange zone (25-45): Fear - Cautious market sentiment
Green (75-100): Extreme Greed - Market may be overbought
Why this matters: Understanding market sentiment helps you contextualize position performance and make better decisions about opening or closing positions.
A numerical indicator (0-100) showing whether Bitcoin or Altcoins are performing better.
Below 50: Bitcoin Season - BTC outperforming altcoins
Above 50: Altcoin Season - Altcoins outperforming BTC
Why this matters: Helps you understand which assets are likely gaining momentum, useful for rebalancing strategies or identifying opportunities.
✅ All capital deployed to liquidity pools
✅ All uncollected fees from liquidity pools
❌ Does NOT include fees you've already claimed
Below the metrics cards, you'll see your complete list of positions with detailed information for each.
Use filters to quickly find specific positions or focus on what matters most. When filters are active, both the position count and dashboard metrics update to reflect only the filtered positions.

Active Filter Indicator When any filter is enabled, you'll see an indicator and the position count will update. For example: "My Positions (4/23)" means 4 out of 23 total positions match your current filters.
Important: Filters also update your Key Performance Metrics cards at the top of the dashboard, showing data only for the filtered positions.
Show positions that are in-range (Active), out-of-range (Inactive), or all positions.
Filter by whether positions are currently open (Active) or closed (Inactive).
All Chains / Ethereum / Optimism / BSC / Solana / Unichain / Polygon / Zksync / Base / Arbitrum / Blast
View positions from specific blockchains only.
Filter to show positions from specific connected wallets. Useful when managing multiple addresses.
Organize your positions by different criteria:
High to Low / Low to High
Sort by annual percentage rate to see your best or worst performing positions.
Sort by position value to see your largest or smallest positions first.
Sort by price range width. Wider ranges = more stable, narrower ranges = more concentrated.
Sort by pending yield to see which positions have the most uncollected fees.
Sort by position age to see recently opened or long-standing positions.
Compact table format, ideal for scanning many positions quickly
Visual cards with more detail, better for reviewing individual position metrics
Any other questions? Get in touch

/invite-codes.title: Invite Codes - DeFi Orbit Docs
/invite-codes.description: DeFi Orbit is currently in beta and available through an invite-only system. This page covers everything you need to know about getting and using your invite code.
h2: How to Get an Invite Code
Benefits of Being an Early Beta User
p: DeFi Orbit is currently in beta and available through an invite-only system. This page covers everything you need to know about getting and using your invite code.
We're taking a different approach to our beta launch for one main reason: offering a personalized experience to our early believers.

Although DeFi Orbit's data is as accurate today as it can be, we don't want to just let our early believers go on their own. We want to briefly meet you, hear your thoughts, prioritize your feedback, and offer a unique and personalized experience.
With direct access to our founders,  for a personalized onboarding session, you get:
Answers to questions about your specific DeFi setup
A walkthrough with someone who knows the platform inside and out
The opportunity to share feedback that directly shapes our product roadmap
Priority support as we refine the experience
This personalized onboarding is only available for the first 100 users. After that, we'll transition to standard onboarding.
The fastest way to get your invite code is to book a 10-minute personalized onboarding session with our founder Davis.
Join the call to get set up and ask any questions. An invite code will be provided.
Start using DeFi Orbit right away
The call is quick, casual, and designed to get you up and running smoothly.
If you prefer to wait, you can join our waitlist at defiorbit.xyz.
Codes are released in batches of 50
You'll receive an email when your code is ready
Batches are released as we onboard users and gather feedback
Not interested in the personalized experience? No problem!
In the coming months, we'll launch publicly without any invite codes or required onboarding calls. You'll be able to head straight to the dApp and start using it on day one.
Launch pricing (won't be available after public release)
The ability to shape the product during its formative stage
As one of our first users, you get:
✅ Launch Pricing - Lock in pricing that won't be available after public launch
✅ Direct Founder Access - Skip the support queue and get help directly from Davis during beta
✅ Shape the Product - Your feedback directly influences our feature roadmap and protocol integrations
✅ Early Access - Test new protocols, chains, and analytics tools before anyone else
✅ Priority Support - Faster response times and dedicated attention during the beta period
✅ Founding Member Status - Recognition as part of the community that helped build DeFi Orbit
Once you have your invite code, here's how to use it:
Go to the dApp - Navigate to app.defiorbit.xyz
Connect your wallet - Choose your preferred sign-in method
Enter your code - Paste your invite code when prompted
Complete signup - Follow the remaining steps (username, yield target, import positions)
Start tracking - Your positions will be imported automatically
For more information on how to use your invite code, please click here.
One-Time Use - Each invite code can only be used once and is tied to a specific account
Expiration Date - Invite codes expire after a set period. Be sure to use yours before it expires
Non-Transferable - Codes are issued to specific individuals and cannot be transferred
Beta Status - During beta, you may encounter bugs or missing features. Your feedback helps us improve
We're rolling out access in waves to ensure every user gets the attention they deserve.
First 100 users get direct founder access
Feature additions based on early feedback
Any other questions? Get in touch

/quick-start-guide.title: Quick Start Guide - DeFi Orbit Docs
/quick-start-guide.description: Get started with DeFi Orbit in just a few minutes. Follow these simple steps to connect your wallet and start tracking your DeFi positions.
h2: Step 5: Set Your Yield Income Target
h3: What If I Have No Positions?
p: Get started with DeFi Orbit in just a few minutes. Follow these simple steps to connect your wallet and start tracking your DeFi positions.
Navigate to app.defiorbit.xyz in your web browser.
We recommend using a desktop browser for the best experience, though DeFi Orbit works on mobile devices as well.
When you first land on DeFi Orbit, you'll see options to sign in.
We use ReOwn/WalletConnect as our wallet connector, giving you multiple ways to access the platform:
Choose the method that works best for you. All options provide the same secure access to DeFi Orbit.
Read-Only Access - DeFi Orbit only reads public blockchain data. We never ask for your private keys or seed phrases, and we cannot execute transactions on your behalf.

Wallet Signature Required - If you sign up using a wallet, you'll be asked to sign a text message. This is NOT a transaction signature—it's simply to verify you own the wallet and prevent anyone from impersonating you or accessing your data. This signature costs no gas fees.
Multiple Wallets - After your initial connection, you can add additional wallets to track your complete portfolio.
DeFi Orbit is currently in controlled release. You'll need an invite code to complete your signup.
Don't have a code yet? Request an invite code here →
Simply paste or type your invite code into the field and click "Continue." Your code will be validated instantly.
Note: Each invite code can only be used once and is tied to a specific account. Invite codes also have an expiration date, so be sure to use yours before it expires.
Pick a unique username for your DeFi Orbit account.
Unique - Only one person can have each username
Alphanumeric - Letters, numbers, underscores, and hyphens allowed
Permanent - Choose wisely—usernames cannot be changed later
Pro tip: Grab your preferred username early. It's yours to keep.
Tell us how much you want to earn from your DeFi positions annually. This is your personal goal, and we'll help you track your progress.
Current annual run-rate - Based on your active positions
Progress toward goal - Visual indicator showing how close you are
Goal tracking widget - Always visible on your dashboard
We calculate your annual run-rate based on your active yield-generating positions:
Enter any amount in USD. Common targets range from $10,000 to $100,000+ per year, but you can set any goal that makes sense for your strategy.
Can I change this later? Yes! Update your yield income target anytime from your dashboard or account settings.
Not sure what to set? Start with a conservative goal based on your current portfolio. You can always adjust as you scale up.
The final step—we'll automatically detect and import your existing DeFi positions.
DeFi Orbit scans your connected wallet(s) and imports:
Uniswap V3 (Ethereum, Base, Arbitrum, Optimism, Polygon, zkSync & BNS)
Aave (Ethereum, Base, Arbitrum, Optimism, Polygon, zkSync & BNS)
For more information on our supported networks and protocols, please click here.
Automatic Detection - We scan your wallet for compatible positions
Position Analysis - We fetch historical data and calculate performance
Dashboard Population - Your positions appear on your dashboard instantly
This usually takes 5-20 seconds depending on how many positions you have.
No problem! You can still explore DeFi Orbit and:
Wait until you open your first position

After signing up, you'll have 7 days of full access to explore all DeFi Orbit features. This gives you time to connect your wallets, import positions, and see how the platform works with your actual portfolio.
Once you complete these steps, you'll land on your Portfolio Dashboard with all your positions visible.
Any other questions? Get in touch

/key-features.title: Key Features - DeFi Orbit Docs
/key-features.description: DeFi Orbit gives you everything you need to track, analyze, and optimize your liquidity positions across multiple chains. Here's what makes our platform different.
p: DeFi Orbit gives you everything you need to track, analyze, and optimize your liquidity positions across multiple chains. Here's what makes our platform different.
Total Portfolio Value - Aggregate value across all positions and chains
Daily Yield - Estimated earnings from all active positions
Pending Rewards - Uncollected fees ready to be claimed
Average APR - Weighted average returns across your portfolio
Investment Value - Your current capital deployed
Liquidity Available - Funds ready for new positions
Why It Matters: No more jumping between different protocols and chains. Get a complete picture of your DeFi investments in seconds, not hours.
To learn more about our Dashboard, please click here.
Track every concentrated liquidity position with precision. Know exactly how each pool is performing and when action is needed.
Current Range Status - See if your position is in-range or out-of-range
Position Health Score - Quick assessment of each position's performance
Fee Earnings - Real-time tracking of collected and uncollected fees
Current APR/APY - Live yield calculations based on actual performance
Token Composition - See your exact token amounts and current values
Position Age - How long you've been in each position
Why It Matters: Concentrated liquidity requires active management. Know when your positions go out of range, when fees are accumulating, and when it's time to rebalance.
Track your lending and borrowing positions across protocols. Never miss a liquidation warning or optimization opportunity.
Health Factor - Real-time liquidation risk assessment
LTV Ratio - Current loan-to-value across all positions
Borrow APY - What you're paying to borrow
Supply APY - What you're earning from lending
Collateral Value - Total value securing your loans
Max Borrow Capacity - How much more you can safely borrow
Why It Matters: Stay ahead of liquidation risks. Optimize your borrowing costs and lending returns with clear visibility into your positions.
To learn more about our Pools' Dashboard please click here.
Manage positions across multiple blockchains without switching between different tools.
Unified portfolio view across all chains
Why It Matters: DeFi is multi-chain. Your portfolio tracker should be too. See everything in one place, make better decisions faster.
Understand your true returns with accurate calculations that account for fees, impermanent loss, and capital changes.
Profit & Loss (P&L) - Realized and unrealized gains/losses
Impermanent Loss Tracking - Real-time IL vs. HODL comparison
Fee Income Analysis - Total fees earned, breakdown by position
Historical Performance - Track returns over time
Position ROI - Returns on each individual position
Net Returns - P&L + fees - IL for accurate performance
Why It Matters: Know if you're actually making money. Many LPs think they're profitable until they properly account for impermanent loss. We show you the complete picture.
Understand your IL exposure and make informed decisions about your liquidity positions.
Current IL - Real-time impermanent loss vs. holding tokens
IL vs. Fees - Are your fees outpacing your IL?
Historical IL - Track how IL has changed over time
IL Projections - Estimate potential IL at different price points
Why It Matters: Impermanent loss is the hidden cost of providing liquidity. See exactly how much it's affecting your returns and whether your positions are still profitable.
Get accurate, up-to-date information with on-demand refreshes. Your dashboard updates when you click "Refresh" to show the latest blockchain data.
Why It Matters: DeFi moves fast. Stale data leads to bad decisions. With DeFi Orbit, you control when to sync the latest information and always work with current data.
Find what you need fast. Filter and sort your positions to focus on what matters most.
Why It Matters: As your portfolio grows, organization becomes critical. Find the positions that need attention and filter out the noise.

/what-is-defi-orbit.title: What is DeFi Orbit? - DeFi Orbit Docs
/what-is-defi-orbit.description: DeFi Orbit is a multi-chain portfolio management platform built specifically for liquidity providers. We help you track, analyze, and optimize your DeFi positions across multiple blockchains—all in one place.
p: DeFi Orbit is a multi-chain portfolio management platform built specifically for liquidity providers. We help you track, analyze, and optimize your DeFi positions across multiple blockchains—all in one place.
If you're actively providing liquidity in DeFi, you know the struggle:
70% of DeFi investors still rely on manual spreadsheets to track their positions
✅ Automatic position import across 10+ chains. Connect once, no manual entry required.
Different chains require different tools, none of which talk to each other
✅ Unified multi-chain dashboard. Track all positions in one interface with aggregate metrics.
By the time you update your spreadsheet, the market has already moved
✅ Real-time updates every 5 minutes. Instant refresh for prices and range status.
No clear view of your total P&L, fee income, or impermanent loss across all positions
✅ Consolidated metrics show total P&L, fees, and impermanent loss across all positions automatically.
Users spend 30-60 minutes daily just trying to understand their portfolio performance
✅ 30-second dashboard check replaces 30-60 minutes of daily tracking. All calculations automated.
⭐️ We built DeFi Orbit because we experienced these problems ourselves as active LPs.
Stop manually checking each protocol. See all your liquidity positions, lending positions, and performance metrics in one unified dashboard that updates in real-time.
No more switching between different tools or maintaining separate spreadsheets for each chain.
Profit & Loss (P&L) - Your actual returns, not rough estimates
Impermanent Loss - Real-time IL tracking vs. fee income
Fee Earnings - Detailed breakdown of all fees collected
APR/APY - Current and historical yields for each position
Understand what's happening with your positions:
In-range vs. Out-of-range status - Know when your liquidity is working
Health scores - Quick assessment of position performance
Historical tracking - See how your positions have performed over time
Lending risk monitoring - Track your health factors and liquidation risks
We're not a generic portfolio tracker trying to do everything. DeFi Orbit is laser-focused on the specific needs of liquidity providers because we are liquidity providers.

After conducting 40+ in-depth interviews with active DeFi users, we identified the exact features that matter most to people managing concentrated liquidity positions, lending markets, and multi-chain strategies.
Read-Only Access - We never ask for your private keys or seed phrases. DeFi Orbit only reads public blockchain data to show you your positions.
No Custody - We don't hold your funds. We don't execute transactions. We simply help you monitor and understand your DeFi investments.
Privacy First - Connect your wallets without KYC. We collect minimal data and focus on giving you the insights you need to make better decisions.
Transform 30-60 minutes of daily spreadsheet work into a 30-second dashboard check.
With accurate, real-time data across all your positions, you can spot opportunities and risks faster.
Know exactly where you stand. No more uncertainty about whether your positions are profitable or if you've missed critical alerts.
Whether you're managing 3 positions or 30, across one chain or five, DeFi Orbit keeps everything organized and accessible.
Any other questions? Get in touch

p: See the latest feature releases, product improvements and bug fixes.

This update introduces comprehensive lending and borrowing tracking with real-time health factor monitoring, LTV ratio visualization, and liquidation risk alerts. Track your Aave positions across all supported chains with instant risk assessment.

Understanding DeFi Orbit's core features, supported protocols, and how we help you manage your multi-chain portfolio.
